description: An access-master record for a business unit / vertical / department. It is the reference target for a user's department and a requisition's department, the scope the approval engine resolves routing rules by, and one of the four RBAC grant sources (vertical permissions). A business unit can carry permissions every member inherits. The same entity serves both the organizational department and the analytics vertical.

fields.name: businessUnitId
fields.label: Business Unit No.
fields.purpose: Stable key a user's departmentId/businessUnitId and a requisition's departmentId foreign-key to so a unit can be renamed without re-keying every member and routing rule.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Business Unit Name
fields.validation: non-blank; unique; e.g. Engineering, Procurement, Flavour Division; the display field for the department/vertical reference and the normalizer (vertical -> name)
fields.purpose: The name shown wherever a department/vertical is referenced (user form, requisition department, routing rule) and resolved by the normalizer; the label spend analytics group by.

fields.name: code
fields.label: Code
fields.validation: non-blank; uppercase; unique; the short code used in code generation and reports
fields.purpose: The short code embedded in identifier patterns (e.g. the requisition DEPT_PREFIX) and used in reports; the machine-friendly key alongside the readable name.

fields.name: type
fields.label: Type
fields.validation: one of the set; distinguishes an organizational department from an analytics vertical or a physical site
fields.purpose: Classifies the unit so routing resolves by department, analytics roll up by vertical, and the site-conditional approval stage (e.g. Factory Manager) keys on SITE units.

fields.name: permissions
fields.label: Permissions
fields.validation: namespaced permission values granted to members of this unit; one of the four grant sources unioned into the effective set
fields.purpose: Permissions every member of the business unit inherits (the vertical grant source); deduplicated into the user's effective set alongside role, user-grant, and designation permissions.

fields.name: parentId
fields.label: Parent Unit
fields.validation: an existing business unit; null for a top-level unit; forms the org hierarchy
fields.purpose: Links the unit to its parent so analytics can roll spend up an org hierarchy and visibility can cascade from a parent department to its sub-units.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: INACTIVE not selectable on new users/requisitions; existing records retain their unit
fields.purpose: Lets an admin retire a dissolved department/vertical from new use without altering users or requisitions that reference it.

invariants: A2 the resolved approval chain is matched by department against routing rules
A13 audit + SSE on every committed change
permissions are one of the four RBAC grant sources unioned into the effective set
